title: NOC 43200 – Sheriffs and Bailiffs | GTR Immigration
description: Sheriffs and Bailiffs under NOC 43200 play a crucial role in Canada’s judicial and legal enforcement system. Classified under TEER 3, this occupation involves court security, enforcement of court orders, serving legal documents, and assisting in maintaining public safety within the judicial process. These professionals work primarily in federal, provincial, territorial, and municipal court systems, and in some cases in private enforcement services.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER3 – Occupations usually require a college diploma of less than two years or more than six months of on-the-job trainingMajor Group43 – Assisting occupations in education and in legal and public protectionSub-Major Group432 – Assisting occupations in legal and public protectionMinor Group4320 – Assisting occupations in legal and public protectionNOC Code43200

duties: Serve court documents such as summonses, warrants, and legal notices
Enforce writs of execution by seizing and selling property
Locate and seize property under federal or provincial legislation
Provide courthouse security for judges and staff
Escort prisoners to and from courtrooms and correctional institutions
Provide security support for sequestered juries
Issue warrants for arrest or imprisonment
Prepare affidavits, reports, and maintain legal records
employment_requirements: College diploma or bachelor’s degree in legal studies is usually required
Sheriffs typically require:Experience as deputy sheriff or bailiffBackground in security or custodial services
Experience as deputy sheriff or bailiff
Background in security or custodial services
Bailiffs usually require:Law enforcement-related experienceKnowledge of relevant statutes and legislation
Knowledge of relevant statutes and legislation
Mandatory provincial training may be required
In Quebec, membership in the provincial regulatory body is mandatory
Background checks and physical fitness standards may apply.
salary_prospects: Sheriffs and Bailiffs typically earn between:
$32,800 – $79,600 per year,depending on province, experience, and level of responsibility.
job_outlook: Enforcement of civil and criminal court orders
Government hiring in public protection roles
labour_market_demand: Sheriffs and Bailiffs are employed by:
Growth in enforcement and compliance requirements
Opportunities in court security and enforcement services
Career advancement to senior sheriff or supervisory roles
Judicial enforcement remains a critical and stable sector across Canada.
pathways: Sheriffs and Bailiffs fall under TEER 3 skilled occupations, potentially qualifying for:
Express Entry (if eligible under skilled worker criteria)
